title: Noida workers protest: Supreme Court tells UP police to produce accused in court amid allegations of custodial torture
description: The Supreme Court on Friday ordered the Uttar Pradesh government to produce before court two men arrested in connection with the Noida protest workers case, after a family member alleged that they were tortured in jail [Keshaw Anand v State of Uttar Pradesh].A Bench of Justice BV Nagarathna and Justice Ujjal Bhuyan has ordered the production of Aditya Anand and Rupesh Roy before the Court on May 18 at 2 PM.Justice BV Nagarathna and Justice Ujjal BhuyanThe Court was hearing a plea filed by Aditya
